info.name: Alraqi ERP System API
info.description:
Complete Postman collection for the Alraqi ERP System (Laravel, modular architecture).

## Modules covered
- **Auth** — login, token refresh, logout, current user (`/api/auth`)
- **RBAC / Roles** — list, create, assign, update, delete roles (`/api/auth/roles`)
- **Notifications** — list, unread count, mark as read (`/api/auth/notifications`)
- **Departments** — full CRUD (`/api/departments`)
- **HR / Employees** — full CRUD (`/api/hr/employees`)

## Getting started
1. Select the **Alraqi ERP - Local** environment (or use the collection variables).
2. Set `baseUrl` (default `http://localhost:8000`).
3. Run **Auth → Login**. Its test script automatically stores `accessToken` and `refreshToken` as collection variables, so every other request is authenticated for you.
4. Call any other endpoint.

## Response envelope
Every response uses a fixed envelope:
- Success: `{ "success": true, "message": "...", "data": {...} }`
- Error:   `{ "success": false, "message": "...", "errors": {...} }`

## Auth model
Bearer tokens (Laravel Sanctum). Access tokens expire in 15 minutes; use **Auth → Refresh Token** to rotate them (the old refresh token is single-use and revoked on refresh).

Default seeded admin credentials (from UserSeeder): `ragab5434@gmail.com` / `MoH.1822`.

exec: // Per-request auth is inherited from the collection Bearer config.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints (prefix `/api/auth`). Login and refresh are public; logout and me require a valid Bearer access token.

exec: // Auto-capture tokens so the rest of the collection is authenticated.
console.warn('No tokens found in login response.');

request.description:
Authenticate with email + password and receive an access/refresh token pair.

**Validation**
- `email` — required, valid email, max 255
- `password` — required, string, 8–128 chars

**Rate limit**: 5 attempts/min per IP (`throttle:auth-login`).

**Success 200** returns `data.access_token`, `data.refresh_token`, `token_type` (`Bearer`), `expires_in` (900s) and the `user` profile. The test script stores the tokens automatically.

Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. The old refresh token is immediately revoked (single-use).

**Validation**
- `refresh_token` — required, string, exactly 64 chars

**Rate limit**: 10 attempts/min per IP (`throttle:auth-refresh`).

Public endpoint (no Bearer needed) — authentication is the refresh token itself.
Revoke all access and refresh tokens for the authenticated user (logout everywhere). Requires a valid Bearer access token.
Return the currently authenticated user's profile. Requires a valid Bearer access token.

item.name: RBAC - Roles
item.description: Role administration (prefix `/api/auth/roles`). All endpoints require a Bearer token plus the relevant permission (`auth.roles.view/create/update/delete`).

request.description: List all roles with their permissions. Requires `auth.roles.view`.

Create a new role. Requires `auth.roles.create`.

**Validation**
- `name` — required, string, max 255, unique in `roles`
- `description` — nullable, string, max 255
- `permissions` — optional array of existing permission **names**
- `permissions.*` — must exist in `permissions.name`

Assign an existing role to an existing user. Requires `auth.roles.update`.

**Validation**
- `user_id` — required, integer, must exist in `users`
- `role_id` — required, integer, must exist in `roles`

Note: the literal `/roles/assign` route is declared before `/roles/{role}` so it always wins. Idempotent — assigning a role the user already holds does not create a duplicate. Call once per role to give a user multiple roles.

Revoke a role from a user. Requires `auth.roles.update`.

**Validation**
- `user_id` — required, integer, must exist in `users`
- `role_id` — required, integer, must exist in `roles`

Idempotent — revoking a role the user does not hold is a harmless no-op (still 200). Any other roles the user holds are untouched. Audited as `role_unassigned`.

Partial update of a role (PUT or PATCH). Requires `auth.roles.update`.

**Validation (all optional)**
- `name` — string, max 255, unique (ignoring this role)
- `description` — nullable, string, max 255
- `permissions` — array of permission names; if present it **replaces** (syncs) the role's permission set
- `permissions.*` — must exist in `permissions.name`

Delete a role. Requires `auth.roles.delete`.

The seeded `admin` role is protected and returns **422** if you try to delete it.

item.name: Notifications
item.description: A user's own notifications (prefix `/api/auth/notifications`). Bearer token required; authorization is ownership-scoped (you only ever see your own).

request.description: List the authenticated user's notifications.
Return the count of unread notifications for the authenticated user (`data.unread_count`).
Mark a single notification (owned by the authenticated user) as read. A notification belonging to another user is not found (ownership-scoped).

item.name: Departments
item.description: Departments CRUD (prefix `/api/departments`). Every endpoint requires a Bearer token and `departments.view` permission. Actions are audit-logged.

query.description: Page number (paginated, 15 per page, newest first).

request.description: Paginated list of departments (15 per page, newest first).

Create a department. Stores the new id into `departmentId`.

**Validation**
- `name` — required, string, max 255
- `manager_id` — nullable, integer (references an employee)
Retrieve a single department by id.

Update a department (PUT or PATCH). On update `name` is optional (`sometimes`).

**Validation**
- `name` — string, max 255 (optional on update)
- `manager_id` — nullable, integer
Delete a department by id.

item.name: HR - Employees
item.description: Employees CRUD (prefix `/api/hr/employees`, registered via apiResource). Every endpoint requires a Bearer token and `hr.employees.view` permission. Actions are audit-logged.

request.description: Paginated list of employees.

Create an employee. Stores the new id into `employeeId`.

**Validation**
- `name` — required, string, max 255
- `phone` — nullable, string, max 50
- `email` — nullable, valid email, max 255
- `address` — nullable, string, max 1000
- `department_id` — nullable, integer, must exist in `departments`
- `job_title` — nullable, string, max 255
- `hire_date` — nullable, date
- `salary` — numeric, min 0
- `status` — one of `active`, `inactive`, `terminated`
Retrieve a single employee by id.

Update an employee (PUT or PATCH). On update `name` is optional; all fields are partial.

Same validation as Create except `name` is `sometimes`.
Delete an employee by id.
